title: Stochastic Attention via Langevin Dynamics on the Modern Hopfield Energy
summary: The paper introduces stochastic attention via Langevin dynamics on the Modern Hopfield Energy, enabling a training-free sampler controlled by temperature. It derives a closed-form entropy inflection condition for the retrieval-to-generation transition and validates the approach on various domains, showing superior performance over learned baselines like VAEs and DDPMs.

title: Matching Features, Not Tokens: Energy-Based Fine-Tuning of Language Models
summary: The paper introduces energy-based fine-tuning (EBFT) for language models, which optimizes a feature-matching objective to target sequence-level statistics of the completion distribution. EBFT uses strided block-parallel sampling and on-policy policy-gradient updates to efficiently generate and process multiple rollouts, providing dense semantic feedback without task-specific verifiers or preference models.

title: A Unified View of Drifting and Score-Based Models
summary: This paper establishes a precise connection between drifting models and score-based models, showing that drifting can be formulated as score matching on kernel-smoothed distributions. Specifically, it demonstrates that Gaussian-kernel drifting is equivalent to score matching on smoothed distributions, using Tweedie's formula, and extends this analysis to general radial kernels, including rigorous error bounds for Laplace kernels.

title: Statistical Inference via Generative Models: Flow Matching and Causal Inference
summary: This paper reinterprets generative AI through a statistical lens, focusing on flow matching as a method for nonparametric learning of high-dimensional probability distributions. It extends score matching to learn transport paths and integrates generative models into statistical inference, maintaining inferential validity through orthogonalization and cross-fitting.

title: Taming Score-Based Denoisers in ADMM: A Convergent Plug-and-Play Framework
summary: This paper introduces ADMM-PnP with the AC-DC denoiser, a framework that integrates score-based generative models into ADMM for inverse problems. The key innovation is a three-stage denoising process that addresses the manifold mismatch between training and optimization geometries, and it provides convergence guarantees under specific conditions.

title: Momentum SVGD-EM for Accelerated Maximum Marginal Likelihood Estimation
summary: The paper introduces Momentum SVGD-EM, an accelerated version of Stein variational gradient descent for maximum marginal likelihood estimation (MMLE), incorporating Nesterov acceleration in both parameter updates and probability measure space. It demonstrates consistent convergence acceleration across various tasks, including high-dimensional settings.

title: Generative Drifting is Secretly Score Matching: a Spectral and Variational Perspective
summary: The paper reveals that generative drifting with a Gaussian kernel is equivalent to score matching on smoothed distributions, providing theoretical insights into the drift operator's behavior and improving its convergence. It also introduces an exponential bandwidth annealing schedule and a new Sinkhorn divergence drift operator.

title: Differentiable Stochastic Traffic Dynamics: Physics-Informed Generative Modelling in Transportation
summary: This paper introduces a physics-informed generative modeling framework for stochastic traffic dynamics, deriving a distributional physics constraint from an Ito-type Lighthill-Whitham-Richards model. The key innovation is the use of a score network with an advection-closure module, trained via denoising score matching and a Fokker-Planck residual loss, enabling distributional traffic-state estimation and stochastic fundamental-diagram analysis.

title: A Variational Latent Equilibrium for Learning in Neuronal Circuits
summary: This paper introduces a biologically plausible framework for approximating backpropagation through time (BPTT) in neuronal circuits, leveraging energy conservation and extremal action principles. It unifies and extends previous approaches to local, time-continuous, phase-free spatiotemporal credit assignment, providing a rigorous foundation for deep learning in the brain.

title: On the Robustness of Langevin Dynamics to Score Function Error
summary: The paper demonstrates that Langevin dynamics is not robust to L^2 (or L^p) errors in the score function estimate, even for simple high-dimensional distributions. This finding highlights the advantage of diffusion models over Langevin dynamics for score-based generative modeling, as diffusion models can sample faithfully from the target distribution under small score function errors.

title: Routing without Forgetting
summary: The paper introduces Routing without Forgetting (RwF), a transformer architecture that addresses online continual learning (OCL) by recasting it as a routing problem. RwF uses energy-based associative retrieval layers inspired by Modern Hopfield Networks to generate dynamic prompts through single-step associative retrieval over transformer token embeddings, enabling input-conditioned routing within each forward pass without iterative gradient refinement.

title: OmniGuide: Universal Guidance Fields for Enhancing Generalist Robot Policies
summary: OmniGuide introduces a flexible framework that enhances generalist robot policies by incorporating diverse guidance sources as differentiable energy functions with task-specific attractors and repellers in 3D space. This approach allows for the integration of complementary guidance sources, significantly improving performance on complex tasks requiring spatial or semantic understanding, manipulation in clutter, or precise manipulation.
